title: Larry Boggs Campaigns With 2019 & 2020 Abortion Rights Men of the Year
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States flags Oklahoma SD7 candidate Larry Boggs for campaigning with the senators they hold most responsible for killing SB13 (the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act), framing the move as evidence that Boggs sides with the senate leadership against abolitionist challenger Warren Hamilton.
audiences: Oklahoma SD7 primary voter weighing Boggs vs. Hamilton
Pro-life Oklahoman trying to understand why SB13 never got a vote
Abolitionist activist tracking which senators block abolition bills
Political reporter covering the 2020 Oklahoma Senate primaries
Pastor or church leader evaluating which candidate to support

questions.question: Which two senators did Larry Boggs publicly campaign with, and why does Free the States consider that newsworthy?
questions.audience: Oklahoma SD7 primary voter weighing Boggs vs. Hamilton
questions.answer: Boggs posted a Facebook photo of himself standing between Senate Pro Tem Greg Treat (the 2019 Abortion Rights Man of the Year) and HHS Committee Chair Greg McCortney (the 2020 honoree) during a McAlester campaign stop. Free the States treats that as a public endorsement of the same leaders they blame for keeping SB13 from a hearing.
questions.quote: Larry Boggs, a candidate for Oklahoma's 7th Senate District, posted a picture on his campaign Facebook page on Friday of himself in between 2019 Abortion Rights Man of the Year Greg Treat and 2020 Abortion Rights Man of the Year Greg McCortney.

questions.question: How did Greg McCortney's role on the HHS Committee allow him to single-handedly stop SB13?
questions.audience: Pro-life Oklahoman trying to understand why SB13 never got a vote
questions.answer: SB13 was assigned to the Senate Health and Human Services Committee, and as chair McCortney had unilateral authority to grant or deny it a hearing. He chose not to schedule one, which prevented the bill from advancing.
questions.quote: State Senator Greg McCortney is the current chair of the Senate Health and Human Services (HHS) Committee, the committee where The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13) was assigned. As committee chair, McCortney had sole discretion over whether SB13 would receive a hearing. It did not receive a hearing.

questions.question: What happened when Joseph Silk tried to pull SB13 out of committee onto General Order?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist tracking which senators block abolition bills
questions.answer: Silk made a motion to bring SB13 to General Order, but Senator Kim David moved to table that motion and McCortney voted with her, killing SB13 for the 2020 session.
questions.quote: When Senator Joseph Silk motioned to bring SB13 out of committee and on to General Order, McCortney voted in favor of [Senator Kim David's motion](https://www.facebook.com/FreeTheStates/videos/549375959015145/) to table Silk's motion and kill SB13 for good; and that's the short version of how abortion was kept legal in Oklahoma in 2020.

questions.question: Why does Free the States hold Greg Treat ultimately responsible for SB13's death even though McCortney cast the deciding vote?
questions.audience: Political reporter covering the 2020 Oklahoma Senate primaries
questions.answer: As Senate Pro Tem, Treat assigned McCortney to chair the HHS Committee and has been the most vocal opponent of SB13 since 2019. The article suggests Treat installed McCortney specifically because McCortney would block SB13.
questions.quote: The man who assigned McCortney to be the chair of the Senate HHS Committee was Oklahoma Senate Pro Tem Greg Treat, the 2019 Abortion Rights Man of the Year. While it was ultimately McCortney's call on whether to hear SB13, Treat has been the primary opponent of SB13 since the beginning of 2019, and likely only gave McCortney the position on the condition that he deny SB13 a hearing.

questions.question: What public arguments has Greg Treat used against abolishing abortion?
questions.audience: Pro-life Oklahoman trying to understand why SB13 never got a vote
questions.answer: Treat has argued that abolishing abortion is unconstitutional, that doing so without permission from the Supreme Court would nullify the US Constitution, that nullifiers are historical villains, and that abolitionists are really pursuing secession rather than saving babies.
questions.quote: Over the past year-and-a-half, Greg Treat has claimed that [abolishing abortion is unconstitutional](https://theresurgent.com/2019/02/17/three-men-keeping-abortion-legal-oklahoma-sb13/) and even that abolishing abortion without permission from the supreme court [nullifies the US Constitution](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-HBoXhPwWYk). He has falsely claimed that [nullifiers are the villains of history](https://theresurgent.com/2019/03/26/are-nullifications-advocates-the-villains-of-american-history/), when in reality, the abolitionists of slavery were the fiercest advocates of nullification.

questions.question: What consequences did Senator Joseph Silk face for championing SB13?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist tracking which senators block abolition bills
questions.answer: Treat stripped Silk of his committee leadership assignment and blackballed most of his bills. Free the States cites this as a chilling effect explaining why other senators won't publicly back SB13.
questions.quote: Treat [punished Senator Joseph Silk](http://tulsabeacon.com/gop-leaders-punish-senator-silk-for-sb13/) severely for fighting for abolition and for his criticisms of the Oklahoma Senate for their refusal to abolish abortion. Silk was stripped of his committee leadership assignment and most of his bills were blackballed.

questions.question: Beyond appearing in a photo with Boggs, how else did McCortney materially support the campaign?
questions.audience: Political reporter covering the 2020 Oklahoma Senate primaries
questions.answer: McCortney transferred $2,800 from his own campaign account to the Boggs campaign, a contribution traceable through Oklahoma's Guardian filing system.
questions.quote: Beyond campaigning with him, McCortney also [donated](https://guardian.ok.gov/PublicSite/SearchPages/FilingAmendmentSelect.aspx?FilingID=146925) $2,800 from his campaign to the Boggs campaign.

questions.question: Why is Free the States backing Warren Hamilton instead of Larry Boggs in the SD7 primary?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader evaluating which candidate to support
questions.answer: Hamilton has pledged to support the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act, which Free the States has formally endorsed. They argue the Treat/McCortney support for Boggs is precisely the proof that Hamilton is the candidate leadership fears.
questions.quote: Warren Hamilton has promised to support The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act and we have [endorsed his campaign](https://freethestates.org/endorsements/index.html). Those like McCortney and Treat, who are doing everything in their power to avoid having to abolish abortion, do not want men like Warren Hamilton in the State Senate.
